active_question: How does Rope365 recommend reducing tripping hazards in the rope-play space?
edited_answer: A bound person may be unable to protect themselves in a fall, and the person tying can also trip while trying to help.
edited_question: Why does Rope365 advise clearing tripping hazards before rope play?
paraphrase_rationale: The replacement explains why clearing the space matters instead of training the tautological answer “clean up.”
reason: The replacement explains why clearing the space matters instead of training the tautological answer “clean up.”
support_evidence: When the hands are tied, one may no longer be able to protect themselves in case of a fall. The person tying can also fall from tripping on an obstacle. Imagine the scenario of someone trying to escape a tie to help their partner unconscious from a fall, scary!
